deploymentOptions.description: Use the hosted GitMCP server via HTTPS URL with placeholders {owner} and {repo} replaced by the target GitHub repository owner and name.

OWNER.description: GitHub repository owner or organization name

deploymentOptions.description: Run the MCP server client using npx with the mcp-remote package. Replace OWNER and REPO in the URL with the target GitHub repository details.

description: GitMCP is a free, open-source MCP server that transforms any GitHub project into a documentation hub, enabling AI tools to access up-to-date documentation and code to reduce hallucinations. It runs in the cloud with zero setup required.
name: GitMCP

tools.name: fetch__7Brepo_7D_documentation
tools.description: Fetch entire documentation file from GitHub repository: %7Bowner%7D/%7Brepo%7D. Useful for general questions. Always call this tool first if asked about %7Bowner%7D/%7Brepo%7D.

tools.name: search__7Brepo_7D_documentation
tools.description: Semantically search within the fetched documentation from GitHub repository: %7Bowner%7D/%7Brepo%7D. Useful for specific queries.

query.description: The search query to find relevant documentation

tools.name: search__7Brepo_7D_code
tools.description: Search for code within the GitHub repository: "%7Bowner%7D/%7Brepo%7D" using the GitHub Search API (exact match). Returns matching files for you to query further if relevant.

page.description: Page number to retrieve (starting from 1). Each page contains 30 results.

query.description: The search query to find relevant code files

tools.name: fetch_generic_url_content
tools.description: Generic tool to fetch content from any absolute URL, respecting robots.txt rules. Use this to retrieve referenced urls (absolute urls) that were mentioned in previously fetched documentation.

url.description: The URL of the document or page to fetch
